commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c4def3738f2ff3cb374de8200e0e480843868dad)
commands.review_summary:
The branch classifies Claude CLI usage rate-limit output as a typed rate-limit error, adds a short background cooldown gate with manual-refresh bypass, preserves prior Claude snapshots for that path, and adds focused parser/fetcher tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and the PR's focused simulated tests: current main treats the Claude CLI rate-limit text as a parse failure, while the PR exercises typed rate-limit and cooldown behavior. I did not run live Claude CLI probes because repository policy avoids live provider validation without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real-behavior proof and a narrow code fix before merge; automation should not be requested while the proof gate remains contributor-owned.

target.title: Handle Claude CLI usage rate limits
target.body:
## Summary
- classify Claude CLI `/usage` rate-limit output as a temporary rate-limit error instead of a parse failure
- pause background CLI probes during a short cooldown while still allowing manual refresh to retry
- preserve prior Claude usage snapshots only for Claude CLI rate-limit failures, without broadening stale-data preservation for other providers

Fixes #1679

## After-fix behavior proof
Redacted local behavior proof from `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 swift test --jobs 1 --filter ClaudeCLITimeoutRetryTests`:

```text
✔ Test "cli usage records rate limit without retrying probe" passed
✔ Test "cli rate limit gate blocks background and allows user retry" passed
✔ Test "manual cli rate limit refresh extends existing cooldown" passed
✔ Test run with 8 tests in 1 suite passed
```

This exercises a simulated Claude CLI `/usage` rate-limit response with no account identifiers or secrets: the parser/fetch path classifies it as `rateLimited`, background refresh is blocked by the cooldown before invoking the CLI again, user-initiated refresh bypasses the synthetic cooldown, and a real user-initiated rate-limit response refreshes the cooldown.

## Tests
- `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 swift build --target CodexBarCore`
- `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 swift test --jobs 1 --filter ClaudeCLITimeoutRetryTests`
- `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 swift test --jobs 1 --filter 'StatusProbeTests|ClaudeCLITimeoutRetryTests|ClaudeOAuthTests'`\n- `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 make check`
target.author: yuxin-qiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
